commands.repair_reason: structured ClawSweeper needs-human verdict with repairable P-severity findings (sha=652c310bd3ac9571000a95166f02a61f77fcaef7)
commands.review_summary:
The PR adds Telegram trailing `notify=false` marker parsing in outbound adapter and native reply delivery paths, adds a shared marker helper, and adds mocked delivery tests.

PR surface: Source +96, Tests +149. Total +245 across 5 files.

Reproducibility: yes. for the PR failure: Mantis Telegram Desktop proof shows the candidate still renders the trailing `notify=false` marker. I did not run a fresh local live Telegram send in this read-only review.
commands.review_followup: - [P1] The blocker is narrow, but this external PR currently has negative real behavior proof; a contributor repair or explicit maintainer-sponsored repair plus passing Telegram proof is needed before merge.

target.title: fix(telegram): consume trailing silent notify marker
target.body:
Closes #80569  ## What Problem This Solves  Fixes an issue where Telegram replies that ended with a standalone `notify=false` delivery marker would still notify users and leak that marker into the visible Telegram message body.  ## Why This Change Was Made  Telegram now consumes only a trailing standalone `notify=false` marker at its delivery boundary, strips it from the sent text, and maps it to the existing silent-delivery option. The marker is normalized before durable text chunking so every Telegram send for that payload inherits `silent`, while inline explanatory text containing `notify=false` remains visible.  Marker-only payloads are suppressed as no-op deliveries instead of fabricating Telegram message identities or attempting empty Telegram sends. The same helper is used by native reply delivery so hook-mutated or native Telegram replies follow the same rule.  ## User Impact  Telegram users no longer see `notify=false` as message text when it is used as a trailing delivery-control marker, and silent-intent replies are sent with Telegram silent notification metadata. Normal Telegram text that merely mentions `notify=false` is unchanged.  ## Evidence  - Duplicate PR gate before implementation:   - `gh search prs --repo openclaw/openclaw --state open --match title,body --limit 20 --json number,title,url,body -- "80569"` -> no open PRs.   - `gh search prs --repo openclaw/openclaw --state open --match title,body --limit 20 --json number,title,url,body -- "notify=false"` -> no direct open fix for this issue. - Focused Telegram delivery coverage:   - `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs extensions/telegram/src/outbound-adapter.test.ts extensions/telegram/src/bot/delivery.test.ts`   - Result: 2 files passed, 98 tests passed. - Heartbeat response-tool regression slice:   - `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/infra/heartbeat-runner.tool-response.test.ts -- -t "treats notify=false as a quiet heartbeat ack|delivers notificationText when notify=true"`   - Result: 1 file passed, 2 tests passed, 14 skipped. - Extension type gates after the no-op payload result typing fix:   - `node scripts/run-tsgo.mjs -p tsconfig.extensions.json --incremental --tsBuildInfoFile .artifacts/tsgo-cache/extensions.local-pr.tsbuildinfo` -> passed.   - `node scripts/run-tsgo.mjs -p test/tsconfig/tsconfig.extensions.test.json --incremental --tsBuildInfoFile .artifacts/tsgo-cache/extensions-test.local-pr.tsbuildinfo` -> passed.
- Deprecated SDK subpath guard for the result-type import repair:
  - `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/plugins/contracts/plugin-sdk-package-contract-guardrails.test.ts -- -t "keeps deprecated public SDK subpaths unused by extension production code"` -> 1 test passed, 21 skipped. - Formatting and whitespace:   - `E:\code\openclaw\node_modules\.bin\oxfmt.CMD --check --threads=1 extensions/telegram/src/silent-marker.ts extensions/telegram/src/outbound-adapter.ts extensions/telegram/src/outbound-adapter.test.ts extensions/telegram/src/bot/delivery.replies.ts extensions/telegram/src/bot/delivery.test.ts` -> all matched files use the correct format.   - `git diff --check` -> clean. - Autoreview:   - `python .agents\skills\autoreview\scripts\autoreview --mode branch --base upstream/main`   - Final result: `autoreview clean: no accepted/actionable findings reported`.  Not tested: a live Telegram bot/group/topic delivery with real credentials. The proof here exercises the actual Telegram adapter/native delivery send seams with mocked Telegram API calls, including marker stripping, silent option propagation, inline-text preservation, durable long-text pre-chunk normalization, and marker-only no-op handling.
target.author: qingminglong
